evals.prompt: I need to write a proposal for adding rate limiting to our API. I have rough notes but no structure yet.
evals.expected_behavior: Classifies this as draft mode, confirms audience or asks for it, and proposes an outline before drafting body content.
assertions: Treats the task as document drafting rather than readability or metadata review
Confirms or asks about the intended audience
Proposes an outline before drafting body sections

evals.prompt: This README is already written. Can you run a readability audit and tell me the Flesch score?
evals.expected_behavior: Classifies the task as readability-audit mode and reports that a Flesch Reading Ease score plus evidence-based prose feedback are the right outputs.
assertions: Recognizes this as a readability audit rather than a drafting task
Mentions a Flesch Reading Ease score
Does not broaden into a plan or prompt rewrite

evals.prompt: Review these repo descriptions and taglines for clarity and truncation fit.
evals.expected_behavior: Classifies the task as metadata-audit mode, reviews the strings as short audience-facing fields, and provides concise rewrites when needed.
assertions: Recognizes the task as short-string review
Provides concrete rewrites instead of vague advice

evals.prompt: Create and approval-gate an implementation plan for the migration before anyone edits code.
evals.expected_behavior: Routes to plan-review rather than treating the request as writing-and-editing work.
assertions: Recognizes that a reviewer-gated implementation plan belongs to plan-review
Does not treat the plan request as a normal document drafting task

evals.prompt: Rewrite this vague request into a repository-grounded execution brief with clear constraints and checks.
evals.expected_behavior: Routes to reverse-prompt rather than handling the request as a reader-facing writing task.
assertions: Recognizes that prompt or execution-brief shaping belongs to reverse-prompt
Does not pretend the request is a normal document or prose audit
